a:r.a:t: MCP stands for Model Context Protocol - it is an open standard that lets LLM agents discover and call tools from external servers dynamically. Our implementation wraps the official MCP Streamable HTTP client with both sync and async support. Security is the primary design concern. Secret encryption uses Fernet symmetric encryption with SHA256 key derivation. The prefix "fernet:v1:" identifies encrypted values, and secrets are never stored in plaintext - they are decrypted only at call time. URL validation enforces HTTPS by default, which is configurable but on by default. Private network blocking prevents SSRF attacks where a malicious MCP server might try to probe your internal network. Connection names are slugified to max 80 characters using only lowercase alphanumeric and underscores. The tool deferral system is a critical safety feature. All MCP tools are registered with defer equals true, destructive equals true, and defer_priority of 50. This means MCP tools only execute after explicit user or agent approval - they never fire automatically. Schema sanitization enforces consistent type definitions on all tool parameters. Connection lifecycle management handles timeouts of 15 seconds for listing tools and 60 seconds for execution. Each tool can be individually enabled or disabled, and status flags gate execution. The McpCatalogService manages the full connection CRUD pipeline plus tool discovery and execution.
